claimText: The River Cities Alliance (RCA) is a joint initiative of the Ministry of Jal Shakti and the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs.
evidenceExcerpt: Joint initiative of the Ministry of Jal Shakti and Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched in 2021.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying the nodal ministries responsible for the River Cities Alliance.
distractorHooks: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change

claimText: Membership to the River Cities Alliance is open, allowing any river city to join at any time.
evidenceExcerpt: Currently has 145 member cities, and any river city can join at any time.
examUse: Statement-based question testing the membership criteria and exclusivity of the alliance.
distractorHooks: Membership is restricted to cities along the Ganga basin
Membership is closed after the initial 145 cities

claimText: The Urban River Management Plan (URMP) Framework was launched in 2020 by the National Institute of Urban Affairs (NIUA) and the National Mission for Clean Ganga (NMCG).
evidenceExcerpt: Urban River Management Plan (URMP) Framework was launched in 2020 by NIUA and NMCG.
examUse: Statement-based question on the institutions behind the URMP Framework.
distractorHooks: Launched by NITI Aayog and Ministry of Environment
Launched by the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB)

claimText: Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar's Kham River Restoration Mission was globally recognised by the WRI Ross Centre Prize for Cities.
evidenceExcerpt: Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar’s Kham River Restoration Mission was globally recognised by the WRI Ross Centre Prize for Cities.
examUse: Correct-pair question matching cities or rivers to their respective restoration missions and global recognitions.
distractorHooks: Recognised by the UN Environment Programme (UNEP)
